ace_test_project_structure.overview: Static reference content for ACE v13 Test Projects: when to create them, the mandatory file structure with ready-to-use templates, naming conventions, and JAR precedence rules. For the procedural workflow (recording, generating, running tests) see 5_ace_test_project_guide.xml.
ace_test_project_structure.when_to_create: Create an ACE Test Project whenever a user asks to "generate tests", "create a test project", or "write unit tests", or when .mxml recorded messages are available. Naming: [AppName]_GeneratedTests — see naming_conventions below.

project_structure.description: An ACE Test Project has the following mandatory file structure. Note: The .project natures and buildSpec are DIFFERENT from an Application project.
project_structure.layout: [AppName]_GeneratedTests/ ├── .project ← Eclipse project descriptor (test natures) ├── .classpath ← Java classpath (JRE + JUnit5 + Integration Test Library) ├── .settings/ │ └── org.eclipse.core.resources.prefs ← Sets UTF-8 encoding; omitting causes "no explicit encoding" warning ├── testproject.descriptor ← References the Application under test ├── build.gradle ← Gradle build using MQSI_BASE_FILEPATH ├── settings.gradle ← Gradle settings: rootProject.name └── src/main/ ├── java/[package]/ │ └── [TestClass].java ← JUnit 5 test class(es) └── resources/ └── [messageId]-[n].mxml ← Recorded message assemblies (from RecordedMessageManager)

file.name: .project
file.description: Eclipse project descriptor for a Test Project. Uses the full ACE Toolkit builder chain and all four required natures. Verified by importing into the ACE v13 Toolkit — this is exactly what the Toolkit writes when it creates a Test Project. Do NOT include Gradle natures — org.eclipse.buildship.core.gradleprojectnature causes an "Unknown referenced nature" warning because Buildship is not installed in the ACE Toolkit by default. The build.gradle and settings.gradle files still work for command-line Gradle builds.
file.critical_note: Use ALL four natures and ALL builders listed below. Confirmed correct on ACE v13 Toolkit. Previous versions of this template were missing: - com.ibm.etools.msgbroker.tooling.testProjectNature (identifies it as a Test Project) - com.ibm.etools.mft.jcn.jcnnature - The full builder chain (only 2 builders instead of 14) - Used wrong Java builder name (mft.java.builder.javabuilder instead of org.eclipse.jdt.core.javabuilder)

file.name: testproject.descriptor
file.description: Links the test project to its Application under test. Replace [AppName] with the exact application project name.

file.name: .classpath
file.description: Java classpath referencing JRE, JUnit 5, and the ACE Integration Test Library. The "Integration Test Library" user library is configured by the ACE Toolkit automatically when the project is imported and the Integration Test Library is installed. Must be created manually — ibmint generate tests does NOT create it. Omitting this file causes the Eclipse Java builder to fail with: "Cannot find the class file for java.lang.Object" "The type java.lang.Object cannot be resolved" These errors clear immediately once .classpath is added and the project is refreshed.
file.template: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <classpath> <classpathentry kind="src" path="src/main/java"/> <classpathentry kind="con" path="org.eclipse.jdt.launching.JRE_CONTAINER"/> <classpathentry kind="con" path="org.eclipse.jdt.junit.JUNIT_CONTAINER/5"/> <classpathentry kind="con" path="org.eclipse.jdt.USER_LIBRARY/Integration Test Library"/> <classpathentry kind="output" path="bin"/> </classpath>

file.name: .settings/org.eclipse.core.resources.prefs
file.description: Sets the project encoding to UTF-8. Without this file the ACE Toolkit shows a "Project has no explicit encoding set" warning. Must be created manually — ibmint generate tests does NOT create it.

file.name: build.gradle
file.description: Gradle build file. MQSI_BASE_FILEPATH env var (set by mqsiprofile) provides the path to the ACE installation. The Integration*.jar and junit*.jar are picked up from there. The rootProject.name MUST match the project folder name.

test_project_naming.note: Update the existing project in place when re-recording; do not create a second project.
BAR file name matches the project name: WeatherAPI_App_GeneratedTests.bar

jar_precedence.description: When multiple locations contain JAR files, ACE uses the following precedence order (highest to lowest). Understanding this is important when troubleshooting ClassNotFoundError or version conflicts in test projects.

levels.level: Application / Library / BAR-embedded JAR — JARs packaged inside the BAR file or referenced by the application/library descriptor

Integration server shared-classes directory: <workpath>/config/<nodeName>/<serverLabel>/shared-classes — Only accessible to that single integration server — Takes precedence over integration NODE shared-classes
Integration node shared-classes directory: <workpath>/config/<nodeName>/shared-classes — Accessible to all servers under that single node — Takes precedence over top-level shared-classes
Top-level shared-classes directory: <workpath>/shared-classes — Available to ALL integration nodes and servers
